title: Define BulletSnapshot + BulletRecord data structures

reference_files.reason: BulletSnapshot - stats Map<ResourceLocation,Double>, traits Map<ResourceLocation,Boolean>, damageType Holder<DamageType>, shooter/gunId/gunInstanceUuid (read-only), state Map, getStat/setStat/getTrait/setTrait/getState/setState methods
BulletRecord - getSnapshot, getShooter, getPosition/setPosition, getDirection/setDirection, getTraveledDistance, getAge, getBulletId
Independent firing snapshot field conventions - shooter depends on param, gunId/gunInstanceUuid always null for fireBullet()

acceptance_criteria: BulletSnapshot class created with: Map<ResourceLocation,Double> stats, Map<ResourceLocation,Boolean> traits, Holder<DamageType> damageType, UUID shooter, ResourceLocation gunId, UUID gunInstanceUuid, Map<ResourceLocation,Object> state
BulletSnapshot provides getStat/setStat, getTrait/setTrait, getDamageType/setDamageType, getState/setState methods
BulletSnapshot provides getShooter/getGunId/getGunInstanceUuid getters (read-only, set only at construction)
BulletSnapshot stats and traits are runtime-mutable (setStat/setTrait callable by trait hooks)
BulletRecord class created with: BulletSnapshot snapshot, UUID shooter, Vec3 position (mutable), Vec3 direction (mutable), float traveledDistance, int age, int bulletId
BulletRecord provides getSnapshot, getShooter, getPosition/setPosition, getDirection/setDirection, getTraveledDistance, getAge, getBulletId methods
BulletRecord position and direction are mutable (setPosition/setDirection for custom trajectories)
BulletRecord age starts at 0, incremented per tick (incrementAge or similar)
Both classes follow code-quality standards (small, focused, under 100 lines each)
